mathematical_rationale: The FRSB (finite-resolution spectral bandlimited) information cut at precision 2^{-k} contains exactly 2^k distinguishable dyadic states. An injective encoding of these states into crossing sequences of length c over alphabet Q requires that the total number of crossing sequences (|Q|^c) be at least as large as the information cut size (2^k). This establishes the fundamental communication bottleneck: continuous bandlimited information cannot exceed the discrete crossing sequence capacity.
lean_4_code_delta:
def FRSB_Information_Cut (k : ℕ) : Type := Fin (2 ^ k)

instance (k : ℕ) : Fintype (FRSB_Information_Cut k) :=
  inferInstanceAs (Fintype (Fin (2 ^ k)))

lemma FRSB_Information_Cut_card (k : ℕ) :
    Fintype.card (FRSB_Information_Cut k) = 2 ^ k := by
  rw [Fintype.card_fin]

structure Crossing_Information_Map (k c : ℕ) (Q : Type) [Fintype Q] where
  encode : FRSB_Information_Cut k → CrossSequenceOfLength c Q
  injective : Function.Injective encode

theorem total_crossing_bound {k c : ℕ} {Q : Type} [Fintype Q]
    (map : Crossing_Information_Map k c Q) :
    (Fintype.card Q) ^ c ≥ 2 ^ k := by
  have h_card_le := Fintype.card_le_of_injective _ map.injective
  rw [cross_sequence_card_bound] at h_card_le
  rw [FRSB_Information_Cut_card] at h_card_le
  exact h_card_le
expected_verifier_state: Definitions `FRSB_Information_Cut` (with cardinality 2^k) and `Crossing_Information_Map` added; theorem `total_crossing_bound` proved, establishing that injective encoding implies (|Q|)^c ≥ 2^k.
